featureType.name: TRB_KEEN_fish_community_responses
featureType.title: Kelp Ecosystem Ecology Network - global fish community responses to kelp abundance
featureType.abstract:
The Kelp Ecosystem Ecology Network (KEEN) conducted a global meta-analysis of underwater visual surveys to determine the effect of kelp bed disturbance and canopy density on the abundance and structure of fish communities.
This data comprises fish abundance, size distribution and (in some cases) feeding behaviour collected by SCUBA divers and underwater video. Fish counts were conducted in control (unmanipulated) plots of rocky reef, and in plots subject to various levels of kelp canopy clearance from human or biological disturbances. Reef habitat type and fisheries management constraints for sampled areas was also recorded.
featureType.nativeCRS: GEOGCS["WGS 84", DATUM["World Geodetic System 1984", SPHEROID["WGS 84", 6378137.0, 298.257223563, AUTHORITY["EPSG","7030"]], AUTHORITY["EPSG","6326"]], PRIMEM["Greenwich", 0.0, AUTHORITY["EPSG","8901"]], UNIT["degree", 0.017453292519943295], AXIS["Geodetic longitude", EAST], AXIS["Geodetic latitude", NORTH], AUTHORITY["EPSG","4326"]]
